title: The Little Bird
content:
My dear Daddie bought a mansion
        For to bring my Mammie to,
    In a hat with a long feather,
        And a trailing gown of blue;
    And a company of fiddlers
        And a rout of maids and men
    Danced the clock round to the morning,
        In a gay house-warming then.
    And when all the guests were gone, and
        All was still as still can be,
    In from the dark ivy hopped a
        Wee small bird: and that was Me.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
